content.full_text:
October 2, 1978

Ms. Maria Contreras  
c/o Assemblyman Montoya  
3617 Monterey Avenue  
El Monte, California 91731

Dear Ms. Contreras:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone, September 28, 1978.

You asked whether or not a corporation would be prohibited from contributing to candidates for state or statewide office in California. Also, if there would be any regulations governing such contributions.

I advised that the Political Reform Act did not contain any restrictions or regulations concerning corporations contributing to state or statewide candidates. You should be aware, however, that if a corporation, or individual contributes $5,000 or more in a calendar year, they would then qualify as a Major Donor pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(c) and, therefore, be required to file campaign reports.

If our office can be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Sincerely,

Helen P. Andino  
Special Compliance Representative
